solutions.name: Tulip Interfaces
solutions.summary: Tulip empowers manufacturers to build custom apps without coding, connecting people, machines, and data on the shop floor.
solutions.whyILikeIt: Tulip nails the balance between flexibility and ease of use. I've seen plants go from paper to digital in weeks, not months. The composable approach means you're not locked into rigid workflows.

ryanScore.comment: Best-in-class for frontline apps. Implementation is genuinely fast if you have internal champions.

solutions.name: Fictiv
solutions.summary: Fictiv is a digital manufacturing ecosystem that makes it faster and easier to source high-quality mechanical parts.
solutions.whyILikeIt: When you need prototype or low-volume parts fast, Fictiv delivers. Their platform handles the complexity of multi-supplier coordination so your team can focus on design, not logistics.

ryanScore.comment: Solid for prototyping and low-volume. Not for high-volume production sourcing.

solutions.name: Augmentir
solutions.summary: Augmentir uses AI to personalize training and work instructions for frontline workers, closing the skills gap.
solutions.whyILikeIt: The AI-driven skills management is a game-changer for plants struggling with workforce turnover. It actually adapts to each worker's proficiency level instead of one-size-fits-all instructions.

ryanScore.comment: Best connected worker platform for skills-focused use cases. AI actually adds value here.

solutions.name: Sight Machine
solutions.summary: Sight Machine provides a unified data foundation for manufacturing analytics, enabling real-time visibility and AI-driven insights.
solutions.whyILikeIt: If you're drowning in data but starving for insights, Sight Machine is your answer. Their data model actually understands manufacturing context, not just generic time-series.

ryanScore.comment: Strong data platform. Requires commitment and good data hygiene to get full value.

solutions.name: Hexagon EAM
solutions.tagline: Enterprise asset management for asset-intensive industries
solutions.summary: Hexagon EAM (formerly Infor EAM) delivers comprehensive asset lifecycle management for complex industrial environments.
solutions.whyILikeIt: Battle-tested in ugly, asset-heavy environments. Not the prettiest UI, but the depth of functionality for serious maintenance organizations is hard to beat.

ryanScore.comment: Deep functionality for serious maintenance orgs. Implementation is not trivial.

solutions.name: Plex Smart Manufacturing
solutions.summary: Plex (now part of Rockwell Automation) delivers a comprehensive cloud MES and ERP for discrete and process manufacturers.
solutions.whyILikeIt: Plex was cloud-native before it was cool. For mid-market manufacturers who need ERP and MES in one platform without the Tier-1 price tag, it's hard to beat.

ryanScore.comment: Best value for mid-market needing ERP+MES. Rockwell acquisition adds stability but watch for enterprise pricing creep.

solutions.name: Limble CMMS
solutions.summary: Limble is a cloud-based CMMS designed for ease of use, helping maintenance teams digitize work orders, PMs, and asset management.
solutions.whyILikeIt: Limble is what happens when you design CMMS for the technician, not the IT department. Fast to implement, actually gets adopted. Perfect for SMB manufacturers.

ryanScore.comment: Best CMMS for SMB. If your maintenance team actually needs to use it daily, start here.

solutions.name: Ignition by Inductive Automation
solutions.summary: Ignition is an industrial application platform with tools for building SCADA, MES, and IIoT solutions—all with unlimited licensing.
solutions.whyILikeIt: The unlimited licensing model changed the game. No more per-tag pricing anxiety. Massive community, incredible flexibility, and you actually own your architecture.

ryanScore.comment: If I could only recommend one platform for plant-floor connectivity, this is it. Unlimited licensing is a revelation.
